full_response:
Step 1.  Identify free ( “hungry” ) vs. dummy ( “fed” ) indices  

A^a B_a = 5  

• The index a appears twice in the same term, once upstairs and once downstairs.  
• By the Einstein summation convention this means we sum over a, so a is a dummy (summed, “fed”) index.  
• Because every occurrence of a is summed, there are no free (“hungry”) indices.

Hence  
free indices: none  
dummy indices: a  

Step 2.  Rewrite with a different dummy index  

A^a B_a = 5  →  A^b B_b = 5  (or A^μ B_μ = 5, etc.).  
Changing the letter does not change the meaning because the index is summed.

Step 3.  Count how many component equations are represented  

A^a B_a = η_ab A^a B^b (in a metric with signature η_ab) is a Lorentz scalar.  
After the summation is carried out one obtains a single number; therefore the tensor equation represents exactly one independent component equation:

A^0 B_0 + A^1 B_1 + A^2 B_2 + A^3 B_3 = 5.  

There is no separate equation for each value of a, because the summation has already been performed.

\boxed{\text{Free indices: none; Dummy index: a;\ Equivalent expression: }A^{b}B_{b}=5;\ \text{Number of component equations: }1}  

Confidence: 86%
